evidence_summary.author: FireworksAI_HQ
evidence_summary.snippet:
How do you go from a blank project to your first fully tested AI agent?

In our latest blog, we share how we adapted Test-Driven Development (TDD) into the LLM era, using evals to define desired behaviors before writing code.

By supercharging Claude Code with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, we grounded the agent in the right context (docs, repos, wikis).

✔️ Faster setup of evals with Eval Protocol
✔️ Richer, more reliable test suites generated automatically
✔️ A safer workflow for iterat

When teams talk about “doing evals,” the hard part is rarely conviction. It’s data.

If you’re running an LLM in production, you already have the most valuable evaluation signal you’ll ever get: real user traffic. The challenge is turning noisy, redundant production logs into a dataset that is small enough to evaluate, yet rich enough to reflect how your system is actually used.

evidence_summary.snippet:
🔥 Excited to announce FireAttention: a breakthrough in the speed vs quality tradeoffs of LLMs

We wrote a custom CUDA kernel optimized for MQA, and FP8/FP16 support on H100. This also lets us run Mixtral at FP8 with negligible impact to quality benchmarks (as opposed to GPTQ, QLORA). When compared with vLLM, the Fireworks stack now serves Mixtral requests ~4x faster than vLLM at FP16, and ~20x faster at FP8.

evidence_summary.snippet:
Guessing Gemini Pro 1.5 uses regular self-attention based on speed.

> Gemini 1.5 Pro successfully completed all the tasks asked of it, but not particularly quickly. Each took between ~20 seconds and a minute to process

Assuming 1M tokens in 60 seconds on a 64-layer model with 8k hidden dimension (like Llama-70B):

4 flops/token * 64 * (1M tokens)^2 * 8k * / (0.7 MFU * ~1k TFlops of H100) / 60 seconds ~= 48 H100

Of course, we don't know model architecture and TPUs are quite different from H100

evidence_summary.author: lqiao
evidence_summary.snippet:
🔥 Day 1/7 -- launching Reinforcement Fine-Tuning 🔥 

Today we're launching Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) Beta on Fireworks AI - an easy, scalable way to train and own your expert open models. With this direction, product and model co-design will be the future norm. 

You can run RFT training by specifying just an evaluator function that scores outputs, and a few examples, with zero infrastructure set up and seamless deployment into production.
💡 With RFT, our users have already:
→ Matched or s

generated_labels.rationale: Heuristic prelabel; requires human review before training.

evidence_checks.notes: Author is an employee posting an explicit product launch announcement for Fireworks AI RFT Beta; provenance and content align with affiliated insider/product launch.

judgment.rationale: The evidence is straightforward: an employee at Fireworks AI announces the launch of RFT Beta on the company’s product. This supports source_role=employee, source_group=affiliated_insider, and content_type=product_launch. No user feedback is being inferred, so feedback_type=none is appropriate.

evidence_summary.author: lqiao
evidence_summary.snippet:
There’s been much discussion recently about comparing the quality of quantized models. Check out our blog on how Fireworks thinks about quantization https://t.co/2C1aRXXlHU

1) There’s no one size fits all for quantization. There are a variety of quantization techniques and potential parts of a model to quantize. Fireworks works closely with customers to tailor quantization to achieve the best quality, cost and speed per use case.

generated_labels.rationale: Heuristic prelabel; requires human review before training.

evidence_checks.notes: Author is an employee posting about Fireworks' quantization approach and linking the company blog; this is affiliated insider technical content, not external customer feedback.

judgment.rationale: The source is clearly an employee account discussing Fireworks' own technical approach and blog content. That supports source_role=employee and source_group=affiliated_insider. The post is technical/proof-oriented rather than feedback or a customer statement, so the generated labels are appropriate.

evidence_summary.author: lqiao
evidence_summary.snippet: We have heard the need for a strong function calling model from many users. I'm thrilled to announce that we have just rolled out the Fireworks-tuned function calling model after several months of work. It is designed to focus on both single-turn and multi-turn contexts, supporting numerous functions, and be a competitive alternative to GPT-4 in this category. As this is an Alpha launch, we still have more to improve. Please try it out and work with us to make it even better!

generated_labels.rationale: Heuristic prelabel; requires human review before training.

The content is a product_launch, and the feedback_type is none because it is marketing/announcement text rather than external customer feedback.
